questions.text: Does your application collect any personal data (e.g., name, IP, or location)?
Is the stored data limited to only the strictly necessary minimum?
Is there an automated cleanup process to delete data once it's no longer needed?
Do you ensure data is not reused for marketing without new consent?
Is sensitive data protected via encryption or strict access controls?
Can users download a copy of their data in a readable format?
Does your system support a complete 'Right to Erasure' (to be forgotten)?
Does your application handle 'Special Category' data (e.g., health data)?
Is the consent mechanism clear, separate, and easy to withdraw?
Do you provide a technical way for users to rectify (fix) inaccurate data?

results.message: It appears GDPR may not apply to this project. Learn why IP addresses still count as personal data.
Data Minimization Gap: You are storing more data than required.
Storage Limitation Gap: Manual deletion processes are a compliance risk.
Purpose Limitation Gap: Data is being reused without justification.
Security Gap: Data must be protected from unauthorized access.
Access Right Gap: Missing a way for users to export their data.
Erasure Right Gap: Systems must support a technical 'Right to be Forgotten'.
Consent Gap: Bundled consent is invalid for sensitive data.
Rectification Gap: Users cannot technically correct their information.
